# ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0018 - QUIC RFC 9000 Application ACK Piggyback Policy Architecture

## Purpose

Describe the bounded active-path 1-RTT Application Data packet-composition policy that piggybacks pending ACK frames onto ordinary ack-eliciting packets and avoids ACK-only feedback loops.

## Scope

This design covers active-path 1-RTT Application Data packet composition only. It does not claim Initial or Handshake packet-builder ACK piggybacking, candidate-path control sends, endpoint interop success, public API behavior, or a general delayed-ACK timer.

## Requirements Satisfied

- REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S13P2P1-0009
- REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S13P2P1-0010
- REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S13P2P1-0012

## Design Summary

The active-path 1-RTT application send helper checks the existing ACK-generation state before protecting an ordinary ack-eliciting Application Data payload. When Application Data ACK state is due for inclusion, it formats only the ACK frame bytes, prepends them to the outgoing payload, protects the composed packet, and records the sent packet with the original retransmittable payload so later 1-RTT retransmission does not replay the non-retransmittable ACK frame. ACK-generation state records the largest ack-eliciting packet number covered by any sent ACK frame, including ACKs carried on ack-eliciting packets, so the same trigger does not create a duplicate ACK-only packet or repeated piggyback loop. The ACK-only send path remains explicitly non-ack-eliciting and does not inject PING or STREAM frames.

## Key Components

- src/Incursa.Quic/QuicAckGenerationState.cs
- src/Incursa.Quic/QuicConnectionRuntime.Protocol.cs
- src/Incursa.Quic/QuicConnectionRuntime.Streams.cs
- tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S13P2P1-0009.cs
- tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S13P2P1-0010.cs
- tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S13P2P1-0012.cs
- benchmarks/QuicAckPiggybackPolicyBenchmarks.cs

## Data and State Considerations

ACK frame composition uses transient packet payload bytes only. Sent-packet tracking stores the protected packet for diagnostics and the original non-ACK plaintext payload for retransmission planning. ACK range retirement remains keyed by the ACK carrier packet number when the piggybacked ACK frame is acknowledged by the peer.

## Edge Cases and Constraints

- Only active-path 1-RTT Application Data sends are in scope for this slice.
- ACK-only packets stay non-ack-eliciting and non-retransmittable.
- A piggybacked ACK frame suppresses duplicate ACK-only sends for the same largest ack-eliciting trigger until a newer ack-eliciting packet arrives.
- A pending ACK is not invented when ACK-generation state has no pending Application Data receipt.
- The original retransmittable plaintext payload is retained for retransmission rather than the ACK-prefixed packet payload.

## Alternatives Considered

- Treat the ACK-generation scheduling tests as sufficient; rejected because the open gap requires packet-composition/runtime proof.
- Use the padded ACK-only payload builder for piggyback composition; rejected because it would insert padding between the ACK frame and the ordinary payload.
- Track the ACK-prefixed plaintext as retransmittable payload; rejected because ACK frames are not retransmittable and retransmission should preserve the original STREAM/control payload.
- Broaden the slice to Initial, Handshake, and candidate-path sends; rejected because the current executable seam is the active-path 1-RTT application send helper and broader packet-space policy needs separate trace.

## Risks

- The supported receive path still has an immediate ACK-only send branch for normal received 1-RTT packets; this slice proves packet composition when pending ACK state reaches an ordinary active-path send, not a full delayed-ACK timer policy.
- Candidate-path application sends continue to use their existing path-specific helper and are not claimed by this active-path composition floor.
- Broader Initial and Handshake ACK piggyback policy remains unclaimed until separately traced.
